title: Kookoskarri köögiviljadega
ingredients: Cooked rice or noodles to serve
instructions: Prepare rice or water for noodles before starting.
Cut vegetables into bite-sized pieces.
Cut tofu into 1cm cubes.
Blanch cauliflower/broccoli in boiling water with a lid for a few minutes.
Heat olive oil in a wok; add carrots, mushrooms, and pepper in sequence.
In a separate pan, fry tofu cubes until crispy; season with salt.
Drain vegetables and add to wok with ginger, kaffir lime leaves, turmeric, and curry paste; stir quickly.
Pour coconut cream over mixture; season with lime juice.
Top with crispy tofu and chopped green onion.
Serve with cooked rice or noodles.
notes: Customize vegetables freely. Substitute coconut cream with coconut milk if preferred. Add curry paste gradually for different spice levels.
description: Aasiahõnguga toit, mida saad vastavalt oma eelistusele täiendada erinevate köögiviljadega. Vali Sulle sobiv vürtsikuse aste või pane ennast proovile! Serveeri riisi või nuudlitega.
descriptionEn: An Asian-inspired dish that you can customize with various vegetables. Choose your preferred spice level or challenge yourself! Serve with rice or noodles.

title: Tomatine läätsesupp
ingredients: Salt, pepper, paprika, garlic powder, rosemary to taste
Fresh herbs (dill, green onion, parsley) for garnish
instructions: Boil 2L water with whole onion, garlic, celery, bay leaves, peppercorns, and bouillon cube.
Dice carrot and add to broth; bring to boil.
Add green lentils.
Prepare diced potato and bell pepper; clean mushrooms.
After 15 minutes of lentil cooking, add potato, pepper, and mushrooms.
Cook 10 minutes; add halved cherry tomatoes.
Remove whole vegetables, peel, dice, and return to soup.
Cook 5 more minutes until lentils are tender.
Season with salt, pepper, paprika, and olive oil.
Garnish with fresh herbs before serving.
notes: Different colored lentils work but require adjusted cooking times. Substitute shiitake mushrooms with any preferred variety.
description: Läätsed ei pruugi olla igaühe lemmikud, kuid see supp annab neile veel ühe võimaluse. Läätsed on kiudainerikkad ja sisaldavad palju rauda ja teisi mikroelemente.
descriptionEn: Lentils may not be everyone's favourite, yet this soup gives them another chance to shine. Rich in fibre and packed with iron and various micronutrients.

title: Paneeritud lillkapsas magushapus kastmes
titleEn: Breaded Cauliflower in Sweet and Sour Sauce
ingredients: 125g wheat flour or GF flour blend
1 medium chili pepper or chili powder to taste
instructions: Preheat oven. Combine batter ingredients (milk, flour, salt).
Separate cauliflower into florets and halve them.
Dip each piece in batter cut-side down. Place on parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake approximately 30 minutes — avoid over-baking to maintain crispness.
For sauce: sauté minced onion in olive oil until translucent. Add garlic, ginger, and sugar; simmer 5 minutes.
Add orange juice, soy sauce, and vinegar. Mix cornstarch with cold water and slowly stir in. Simmer until thickened. Add sesame oil.
Transfer cauliflower to serving dish, pour sauce over top, and garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.
notes: Use cornstarch only — potato starch becomes sticky with prolonged heating. Pour sauce immediately before serving to preserve crispness.
description: Krõbedad lillkapsa "nagitsad" klassikalises magushapukastmes maitsevad ehk kõige paremini valge riisiga. Sobib hästi ka suuremale seltskonnale pakkumiseks.
descriptionEn: Crispy cauliflower florets in classic sweet and sour sauce taste best with white rice. This dish works wonderfully for serving larger groups as well.

title: Kapsarullid
instructions: Cut around the cabbage root and boil the head in water until leaves soften. Remove carefully and cool.
Cook rice in 1.5x volume of water. Dice onion and garlic; sauté until translucent.
Add thawed vegan ground meat to onion mixture. Stir in cooled rice and seasonings.
Remove 10 leaves from cooled cabbage. Cut out tough center ribs. Place filling in each leaf and roll tightly.
Brown rolls in oil on both sides. Dissolve bouillon cube in boiling water (~0.5L) and pour over rolls.
Cover and simmer on low heat until tender, adding water if needed.
notes: Leftover cabbage works for salads or stews. Alternative cabbages work (kale, Napa cabbage). Can use frozen or dried vegan meat alternatives.
description: Maitsvaid kapsarulle saab edukalt teha ka ilma lihata. Varu natuke rohkem aega, et saaksid rahulikult meisterdada – su pingutus on seda väärt!
descriptionEn: Delicious cabbage rolls made without meat. Set aside extra time so you can work at a relaxed pace — your effort is worth it!

title: Kartulikuhjad
instructions: Wash and coarsely grate the potatoes. Season with salt, crushed garlic, and optional potato seasoning. Mix until uniform.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and spray with olive oil. Shape the potato mixture into mounds and drizzle with additional olive oil.
Bake at 220°C for about 30 minutes until golden brown.
Top with fresh chopped herbs before serving.
notes: Mix fresh herbs directly into the potato mixture if preferred. Results vary depending on potato variety.
description: Kui oled kartulit juba igal võimalikul viisil valmistanud, siis proovi seda lihtsat vaheldust. Sobib lisandiks paljudele toitudele ja maitseb ka neile, kes kartulit väga ei armasta.
descriptionEn: If you've prepared potatoes in every conceivable way, try this simple variation. Works as a side dish and appeals even to those who don't particularly love potatoes.

title: Kodune suitsutofu
instructions: Mix water and salt to prepare brine, then submerge tofu blocks completely.
Place the container in a cool location for 2–3 days.
Cut cheesecloth into appropriate pieces and individually wrap each tofu block.
Use single layer for stronger smoke flavor or double layer for milder taste.
Process using a smoking apparatus according to preference.
notes: Use as sandwich filling or substitute smoked tofu in salads, tortillas, wraps, or patties. Works well for grilling.
description: Suitsutofu on nii geniaalne leiutis, et viib lihtsalt keele alla! Kui sul juhtumisi on suitsuahi, siis parima suitsutofu saad muidugi ise suitsutades.
descriptionEn: Smoked tofu is such a brilliant invention that it simply melts in your mouth! If you have a smoker, you can make the best smoked tofu yourself.

title: Aedoad köögiviljadega
ingredients: 1 large carrot or young mini carrots
instructions: Cook fresh beans for 15–20 minutes until tender; frozen varieties require slightly longer.
While beans cook, lightly fry carrot pieces in a pan. Once carrots absorb color, add halved mushrooms and cook until browned.
Add halved cherry tomatoes and cooked beans to the pan. Stir quickly, allowing everything to brown slightly.
Add vegetable broth concentrate or powder. Stir and taste after a few minutes.
Season with thyme and black pepper.
Serve with rice, potatoes, or alone.
notes: Avoid using bean cooking water due to phytates. For dried beans: soak overnight, changing water periodically; cook early the next day (may take up to 1 hour).
description: Rustikaalne ja valgurikas pannitäis! Seenehooajal kasuta metsaseeni – saavutad veelgi pikantsema maitse!
descriptionEn: A rustic, protein-rich skillet dish. During mushroom season, use wild mushrooms for an even more flavourful result.

title: Bataadi-peedisalat
instructions: Create thin spirals from sweet potato and beet using a spiralizer (or coarse grater).
Combine all dressing ingredients in a bowl.
Toss the spiralized vegetables with the dressing.
Serve topped with chopped green onion and roasted pumpkin seeds.
notes: Raw beet and sweet potato contain abundant vitamins and minerals. Pumpkin seeds provide essential minerals.
description: Kiirelt valmiv värskendav salat sobib lisandiks või ka omaette söögikorraks. Toores peet ja bataat sisaldavad rikkalikult vitamiine ja mineraalaineid.
descriptionEn: A quick, refreshing salad that works as a side dish or standalone meal. Raw beets and sweet potatoes are rich in vitamins and minerals.

title: Tacokausid
ingredients: 150g salsa sauce, chili sauce, or tomato puree
instructions: Dice tomatoes into bite-sized pieces; mince onion and garlic.
Heat onion in olive oil until slightly translucent, then add garlic. Season with black pepper.
Rinse canned beans thoroughly and drain corn.
Add beans and corn to the pan, stirring well. Once simmering, add salsa sauce. Season with salt and paprika.
Layer spinach in taco shell bottoms, then add the filling. Reserve space for fresh tomato pieces.
Top with grated vegan cheese and bake at 200°C until shell edges brown.
Garnish with fresh basil or parsley before serving.
notes: Tomato sauce is a milder alternative when salsa is unavailable. The same filling suits traditional taco shells.
description: Tacokausid on heaks alternatiiviks klassikalistele tacolõõtsadele. Pealegi hoiavad need täidetuna paremini tasakaalu ja täidis ei vaju küpsedes välja.
descriptionEn: Taco bowls are a great alternative to traditional taco shells. They hold their balance better when filled and the filling doesn't fall out during cooking.

title: Läätsebolognese
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan at medium temperature.
Place diced onion on one side and chopped tomato and pepper on the other; cook 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic to the onion side and mix through; cook until onion becomes translucent.
Add lentils to the tomato mixture and stir through.
Cook another 5 minutes, then combine both sides; add tomato puree.
Incorporate soy granules; add water if needed to prevent drying.
Season with ketchup, chili sauce, soy sauce, and spices; let simmer.
Cook until lentils are tender; adjust consistency with water as needed.
Garnish with fresh basil and drizzle with olive oil.
notes: Balance four flavor profiles: saltiness, sweetness, acidity, and spice. Use different spice blends for Asian, Creole, or Italian versions.
description: Valgurikas klassikalise Bolognese kastme analoog. Sobib serveerimiseks riisi, makaronide, kinoa, hirsi või tatraga.
descriptionEn: A protein-rich alternative to classic Bolognese sauce. Works well served with rice, pasta, quinoa, millet, or buckwheat.

title: Ahjubataat
ingredients: Fresh sage leaves or other herbs
instructions: Wash and halve the sweet potatoes. Place them cut-side up on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Top the cut surfaces with sage leaves. Season with salt and black pepper, then drizzle generously with olive oil.
Cut the tops off garlic heads, drizzle with olive oil, and place among the potatoes on the sheet.
Roast in a 220°C oven for approximately 40 minutes, or until tender when pierced with a knife.
When serving, squeeze the roasted garlic from its skin and spread onto the sweet potato flesh.
Serve with fresh salad or preferred accompaniments.
notes: Leftover bataat keeps well — dice and add to salads the next day, or blend with avocado to make a dip.
description: Superlihtne ja väga maitsev toit! Bataadist saad rikkalikult A-vitamiini ja mineraalaineid.
descriptionEn: Exceptionally simple and delicious! Sweet potato provides abundant vitamin A and minerals — and even sweet potato sceptics tend to love it this way.

title: Lillkapsanagitsad
ingredients: 100g soy milk, oat milk, or water
instructions: Mix flours and seasonings; add liquid and stir until smooth. Let batter rest while preparing cabbage.
Separate florets from the cabbage head. Halve each floret, then trim the rounded side to create ~1cm thick 'steaks'.
Check batter consistency — it should resemble sour cream. Adjust with flour or water.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Coat each cabbage piece in batter, then dredge in millet flakes.
Fry cabbage pieces until golden, turning occasionally and adding oil as needed. Cook until tender.
Season hot fritters with additional garlic powder or preferred spices.
notes: Substitute cornflour and oat flour with chickpea or wheat flour. Use as a chicken substitute in salads or wraps.
description: Krõbedad lillkapsanagitsad sobivad nii dippimiseks kui lisandiks paljudele toitudele.
descriptionEn: Crispy cauliflower fritters that work well as a dipping appetizer or as a side to many dishes.

title: Pitsataigen
instructions: Mix yeast with sugar and add 0.5 tsp warm water; let rest briefly.
Sift flour into bowl; add salt and mix.
Make a well in the flour center; pour yeast mixture and water into it; add oil.
Using a fork, gradually incorporate flour from well edges until dough forms.
Knead dough on work surface for ~5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place in floured bowl, dust top lightly, cover with cloth.
Let rise ~1 hour in a warm place (or 30°C oven).
Knead briefly on floured surface and shape into pizza base.
notes: Substitute portion of wheat flour with whole grain or buckwheat flour if desired. Make multiple portions and freeze dough balls wrapped in plastic for later use.
description: Klassikaline pitsataigen nisujahuga. Soovi korral asenda osa jahust täisteranisujahuga või tatrajahuga.
descriptionEn: A classic pizza dough made with wheat flour. Optionally substitute part of the flour with whole grain or buckwheat flour.

title: Kõrvitsapüreesupp
ingredients: 300g butternut squash or other pumpkin variety
Olive oil or pumpkin seed oil
instructions: Peel and dice squash and carrot into 2cm pieces. Add to pot and cover with water.
Dice onion, garlic, ginger, and chili; sauté in olive oil, starting with onion then adding the rest.
Add curry powder and turmeric (~0.25 tsp each) and black pepper.
Transfer pan mixture to pot with vegetables; add bouillon.
Toast pumpkin seeds in a dry pan over low heat until golden.
Simmer until vegetables are tender.
Blend soup with immersion blender, adding coconut milk gradually until silky.
Return to heat and bring to a simmer.
Season with salt, pepper, or cayenne as needed.
Serve topped with toasted seeds, herbs, and an oil drizzle; serve with garlic-rubbed ciabatta.
notes: Butternut and Hokkaido varieties produce the most flavorful results. Coconut milk may be substituted with soy or oat-based alternatives.
description: Sügisene kindel lemmik! See supp toob kogu pere laua äärde kokku ja kindlustab mõnusa olemise.
descriptionEn: An autumn favourite that brings the whole family together around the table and guarantees a cosy, warming time.

title: Köögiviljawok
ingredients: Salt, pepper, garlic powder to taste
Rice, quinoa, buckwheat, millet, or noodles to serve
instructions: Clean and chop vegetables. Prepare accompanying grain simultaneously.
Boil peas in a small pot for ~10 minutes with 0.5 tsp salt.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil on low heat in a pan.
Add carrots, cook on high heat ~5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add cauliflower, mix with carrots, heat several minutes.
Add broccoli, season with salt, pepper, garlic powder.
Add ~50g water, cover pan, simmer ~10 minutes on low heat.
Add cooked peas, mix thoroughly, cook covered a few more minutes.
Garnish with fresh herbs, serve with chosen grain.
notes: Vegetable selection is flexible. Frozen vegetables work — add simultaneously and extend cooking time as needed.
description: Lihtne kuid toekas pannitäis kiudaineterikast toitu teeb sinu kõhule pai. Kiudained toetavad seedefunktsioone ja toidavad kasulikke baktereid.
descriptionEn: A simple yet nourishing pan of fibre-rich food that benefits your digestive system and feeds the good bacteria.

title: Supipuljong
ingredients: Vegetable bouillon cube or seasoning paste (optional)
instructions: Clean and prepare vegetables, then add to boiling water.
Skim off foam that rises during initial boil.
Add bay leaves, spices, and remaining ingredients.
Simmer gently on medium heat for approximately 1 hour.
Strain through a fine sieve or remove vegetables with a slotted spoon once ready.
Store in refrigerator for several days.
notes: Use any available vegetables — this is an excellent way to repurpose scraps. Essential basics are carrot, onion, bay leaf, and spices. Keep pot uncovered for clear broth.
description: Sobib iga supi põhjaks. Kogu nädala jooksul kokku kõik brokkoli- ja lillkapsajuurikad, sellerilehed ja porrukontsud ning keeda neist maitsev puljong!
descriptionEn: A foundation for any soup. Collect vegetable scraps throughout the week — broccoli stems, cauliflower cores, celery leaves, leek ends — and transform them into a flavourful zero-waste broth.

title: Tofu-oakotletid
ingredients: 1 bouillon cube or 1 tbsp bouillon powder
instructions: Drain and rinse canned beans thoroughly. Mash them until a pudding-like consistency.
Use soft tofu or grate firm tofu with a coarse grater; add to mashed beans.
Finely chop onion and garlic; mince jalapeños and green onion. Add all to mixture.
Add cornstarch and seasonings; knead until uniform dough.
Form cutlets of desired size.
Fry in hot oil on medium-low heat; flip after a few minutes. Add more oil as needed.
notes: Reliable plant-based cutlets that hold together well. Serve with potatoes or inside burgers.
description: Need kotletid on tõelised valgupommid, sisaldades endas taimseid valgurikkaid koostisosi tofut ja ube. Sobivad tavapärase 'kartul-kaste' roa juurde aga ka burgeri vahele.
descriptionEn: These patties are true protein powerhouses, containing protein-rich tofu and beans. Perfect alongside potato dishes or tucked inside a burger.

title: Pasta köögiviljadega
ingredients: 150g spiral pasta or any pasta (dry weight)
instructions: Cut zucchini into 2–3cm pieces. Separate broccoli into florets; halve cherry tomatoes. Mince onion and garlic.
Boil water for pasta.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil on medium. Add tomatoes, sauté briefly, season with pepper. Add onion and garlic; mix.
Begin cooking pasta in boiling salted water.
Add zucchini and broccoli to pan; heat on medium while stirring for several minutes. Cover and simmer until tender.
Combine cooked pasta with vegetables. Garnish with basil or preferred herbs.
notes: Prepare extra portions for easy reheating. Choose whole wheat pasta for enhanced vitamins and fiber.
description: Miks mitte tavapäraseid makarone veidi tervislikumaks muuta!? Selles retseptis on kasutatud türgiube, brokkolit ja tomatit, kuid sobivad ka teised köögiviljad.
descriptionEn: Why not make ordinary pasta a bit healthier? This recipe uses beans, broccoli, and tomato, but other vegetables work just as well.

title: Vietnami kevadrullid
ingredients: Soy sauce mixed with sweet chili sauce for dipping
instructions: Chop or julienne vegetables; arrange in separate bowls on a large plate.
Pour 2–3 cm of hot water into a shallow bowl.
Submerge rice paper sheet in hot water for about one minute, then transfer to plate.
Layer vegetables on one edge of the sheet.
Fold sides inward over filling, then roll tightly toward the end.
Dip completed roll into sauce and serve.
notes: Try sweet Vietnamese rolls with fruits, berries, sweet sauces, and melted chocolate as alternatives.
description: Milline värskendav amps! Valmib kiirelt ja valutult! Valmista ette kausikesed erinevate täidistega ja igaüks saab valmistada endale meelepärased rullid.
descriptionEn: What a refreshing bite! Quick and effortless to make. Prepare bowls with various fillings so everyone can roll their own.

title: Seenerisotto
instructions: Wash cherry tomatoes, place on parchment-lined baking pan, drizzle with olive oil, season with pepper, salt, and thyme. Bake at 180°C for 30–40 minutes.
Rinse rice under cold water, cook in 1.5x water for 10 minutes, then set aside.
Dice mushrooms, chop onion and garlic finely. Heat broth.
Sauté mushrooms in hot oil until half-cooked. Add onion, cook briefly, then add garlic.
Add white wine and stir. Add rice, gradually incorporate broth while stirring constantly over low heat.
Season with salt and nutritional yeast. Risotto is ready when rice is tender but has slight resistance.
Serve topped with nutritional yeast, grilled tomatoes, fresh black pepper, and a drizzle of olive oil.
notes: Use a higher-sided pot to reduce evaporation. Can substitute hot water if broth runs out. Works with sun-dried tomatoes too.
description: Kukeseened, puravikud, metsaseened, šampinjonid, portobelloseened – kõik need sobivad ideaalselt selle risotto valmistamiseks. Söök, mis maitseb kõigile.
descriptionEn: Chanterelles, porcini, wild mushrooms, cremini, portobello — all work perfectly in this risotto. A dish that appeals to everyone, from weeknight dinners to intimate evenings for two.

title: Toortatar glasuuritud kõrvitsaga
ingredients: Masala or other seasonings to taste
instructions: Rinse buckwheat thoroughly and bring to boil with 1.5x water volume and a pinch of salt. Cook for 20 minutes.
Cut unpeeled Hokkaido pumpkin into 1–1.5 cm slices; remove seeds. Strip kale leaves from stems and chop. Slice carrot into strips.
Place pumpkin slices on a hot pan and lightly brown over medium heat. Flip and drizzle with soy sauce and chili sauce. Season with masala.
Transfer finished pumpkin to a bowl and cover. Sauté carrot strips and kale lightly in oil. Season with salt, pepper, and minced garlic. Cover and braise a few minutes.
Combine all components and serve.
notes: Hokkaido pumpkin works best; regular varieties may become watery. If kale isn't preferred, chop finely and braise longer.
description: Tatar on väekas tooraine, mis sisaldab hulgaliselt valguaineid. Glasuuritud kõrvits on pisut vürtsikas, kuid samal ajal magus, ning teeb selle lihtsa roa lihtsalt vastupandamatuks!
descriptionEn: Buckwheat is a powerhouse ingredient packed with protein. The glazed pumpkin adds gentle spice and sweetness, making this simple dish completely irresistible.

title: Värskekapsahautis
instructions: Set potatoes to boil.
Slice fresh cabbage into approximately 2cm strips and halve them. Dice onion and mince garlic.
Heat olive oil in pan and lightly fry the vegan ground meat.
Add onion to pan, then garlic after several minutes. Stir and add cabbage.
Crumble in bouillon cube and season with meat flavoring and salt. Add 0.5 cup water and simmer covered on low heat.
Monitor liquid levels to maintain moisture. Garnish with fresh dill.
notes: Substitute regular cabbage with savoy or red cabbage. Avoid reducing all cooking liquid — maintain adequate sauce consistency.
description: See vana klassika… Uskumatu, aga see saab superhea ka ilma hakklihata! Kasuta kuivatatud sojamassi või mistahes vegan hakist. Serveeri koos keedetud kartuli või kartulipudruga.
descriptionEn: This old classic turns out amazingly well without any meat. Use dried soy crumbles or any vegan mince, and serve with boiled potatoes or mash.

title: Glasuuritud pudelkõrvits Hasselbacki kartulitega
titleEn: Glazed Bottle Gourd with Hasselback Potatoes
instructions: Peel and halve the gourd lengthwise. Place sushi sticks alongside to guide the knife, then carefully slice into thin leaves (~1.5–2 cm) without cutting through completely.
Mix glaze ingredients (chili sauce, soy sauce, pumpkin spice, paprika, olive oil, pepper) and brush generously over gourd halves.
Peel potatoes and slice with ~0.5 cm intervals. Coat with olive oil, herbs, and salt. Arrange beside gourd.
Brush gourd with glaze every 15 minutes. Bake approximately 45–55 minutes.
Final minutes: raise oven to grill/broil setting briefly. Monitor carefully.
Steam asparagus in final 15 minutes. Season lightly with salt and garlic powder.
notes: This is a showstopper centrepiece dish. Monitor closely under the broiler to prevent burning.
description: See söök valmib ahjus ja vajab vaid pisut ettevalmistusaega. Sobib klassikalise lihaprae asenduseks sünnipäevapeole või miks ka mitte jõulupraeks.
descriptionEn: Oven-baked with minimal prep time. An impressive substitute for a traditional meat roast — perfect for a birthday celebration or festive holiday dinner.
